Erico has been able to build out his service with the help of hundreds of volunteers and a steady stream of financing from a foreign investor known as Shylock Jones. Erico expects that he will be able to unveil his service in the coming months. In preparation for Skynet's coming-out party, Erico has registered a private company by the name of Skynet Holdings Limited in which Erico serves as the sole director/shareholder. Erico's friend and fellow IT enthusiast Stella recently caught wind of the project and she is interested in coming on board as a director and majority shareholder in Skynet Limited.

a) Stella and Erico have paid you a visit and instruct you to prepare the relevant documentation to add Stella as a director and shareholder of Skynet. Briefly outline the paperwork to be lodged with the registrar to effect these changes. (5 marks)
b) As a condition to being a part of the Skynet enterprise, Stella, with the assistance of her well-off family injected capitation into the company to the tune of Kenya Shillings One Hundred Million (Kshs. 100,000,000/=). Erico holds a duly registered copyright over the Skynet Al in his favor and he has even signed a security agreement in favor of Stella in her capacity as a director of Skynet Limited to ensure that the service remains under the control of Stella and the company for the foreseeable future. Stella now seeks your advice on how to perfect her security right under the movable property security rights (MPSR) regime and whether said security rights extends to all the anticipated future proceeds for Skynet. Please advise her. (4 marks)
c) As expected, the Skynet Al becomes a resounding success and the company licenses out the Al to several companies and even the government. With several income streams available to them, Erico and Stella are kept very busy managing their product and the company's finances. In a bid to get her house in order, Stella visits you once again and admits that even before she perfected her security in Skynet, she knew of the existence of a security right in favor of Shylock Jones but the same was never perfected by Mr. Jones. Advise Stella on whether Shylock Jones has a claim against her or the company in terms of MPSR priorities and whether her knowledge of Mr. Jones' right affects her interest in any way and if she could claim to be an innocent purchaser for value? (4 marks)
d) Several years after the success of Skynet, Erico informs you that his success went to his head and he made several bad financial decisions. As a result, he has been adjudged a bankrupt pursuant to an adjudication order. In spite of his status as a bankrupt, Erico sought out Shylock Jones once again and borrowed some money from him and in exchange, surrendered all the administrative rights, share files and permissions to the Skynet Al to Shylock Jones who is now in control of the service. Erico also admits that he has misrepresented his true financial position in writing to several of his creditors including his business partner Stella. Analyze and explain to Erico his guilt or otherwise under our bankruptcy laws in relation to debts and making of documents by a bankrupt. (7 marks)

Lucia and Brian are the respective Chairperson and Secretary of LBC Company LTD, which has five directors. The company was doing well for a long time since its incorporation in 2002. However, due to COVID 19 pandemic, its operations went down considerably leading to massive loss of business and revenue. Consequently, the company has been unable to pay its debts to its creditors. They have tried everything possible to revive the business, but things seem to be getting worse and worse. The two have come to your law offices for legal advice on what they need to do to avoid further losses and lawsuits from the creditors. They have been musing on the possibilities of liquidating the company. Citing relevant provisions of the law advise them on the options available including the following:

a) The three legal options available under the Kenyan law for companies struggling with debts and threat of suits from creditors? (6 marks)
b) The powers of the insolvency administrator under the law of insolvency? (2 marks)
c) Two qualifications of an insolvency practitioner under Kenyan law. (2 marks)

a) Luku Poa is a famous accessories and fashion brand operating in the country. The brand is especially famous with campus female students because of their high quality and affordable prices. Brande is a student in one of the local universities. She has an entrepreneurial spirit which has led her to establish a business in accessories and fashion for women. Brande recently approached Luku Poa and requested to use their brand for her business. Luku Poa considered her request and after a brief investigation considered her to be a good fit since she already has a strong customer base among the university female community. Brande is excited about the opportunity and in order to move things forward has approached your chambers to draft a franchise agreement on her behalf so that she can use it to negotiate the details of her relationship with Luku Poa. Draft a franchise agreement covering the basic elements only. (5 marks)
b) Brande's business partner Phela has opposed the decision by Brande to enter into a business relationship with Luku Poa. According to Phela, their business with Brande was fast gaining popularity and would have been the perfect competition for Luku Poa in Kenya. Phela argued that the resources being used to enter into the franchise agreement with Luku Poa could best be utilized to develop their partnership. After in-depth discussions, the two partners have decided to approach the court for the dissolution of their partnership so that the two partners can pursue their business interests separately. The partners have requested you to write a comprehensive legal opinion on the instances in which the courts may decree a dissolution of a partnership taking into account to discuss their unique circumstances in each case. (5 marks)

Raha Entertainment and Jamii TV have been in talks to streamline their respective businesses. Raha Entertainment operates a widely watched TV station focused on family entertainment. Raha is also a new entrant into the TV streaming business and is in the market for content to populate said streaming service dubbed "Raha One." Jamii TV on the other hand has a vast library of locally produced content but has in recent years favored the production and airing of more mature programming and is seeking to do away with its library of children content. As a result, Jamii found a willing partner in Raha to purchase its star animated children's' program - The Zola and Udi Show together with its offshoots, The Zola and Udi Magic Show and Zola and Udi in the Jungle. Jamii considers the children's show as a valuable piece of its intellectual property (IP) portfolio as they have been able to monetize the said characters by selling t-shirts, toys and books related to the show's characters. Raha is aware that adding the show to their content library may prove advantageous to their streaming business model which is a pay-per-view service set to be launched soon. Raha's assets/turnover (whichever is higher) does not exceed USD 3 million while Jamii's threshold is USD 1 million. Raha is only interested in acquiring the one TV program from Jamii.

a) Raha's CEO has been in talks with Raha's board and other advisers and he has been informed that the proposed merger may be notifiable. Briefly explain to Raha the three (3) categories of mergers (relevant to these facts) in terms of the Competition Authority of Kenya's (CAK) threshold guidelines and where Raha's proposed merger would fall. (4 marks)
b) You are further informed that Jamii prefers an outright sale of its Zola and Udi property versus a shares acquisition by the acquirer. With the foregoing in mind, highlight four (4) important areas to be targeted at the due diligence stage of this transaction. Assume that you act for the acquirer. (4 marks)
c) In terms of the asset purchase, Raha's CEO makes it very clear to you that his company is very keen on two (2) considerations with respect to the asset purchase - "selective acquisition" and "liability control." Analyze these two concepts vis-à-vis the companies' visions as highlighted in the fact pattern. (2 marks)

a) Francis, the director of Tag Masters Ltd, has decided to sell his residential property where he has lived for the last seven years. The reason for this sale is to offset a bank loan and downsize to a smaller property now that his children have left the nest. His friend Joe, who runs Group of Schools Ltd has agreed to buy the property through his company Group of Schools Ltd. The sale price of the residence is Kshs. 50 million shillings. Citing the relevant statutory provisions, advise Francis on whether he is liable for any tax payment should the transaction proceed. (3 marks)
b) Rustic Acres Ltd would like to sell 49 acres of land for the price of Kshs. 1 billion. Your Client Crafty is the director of Rustic Acres Ltd. Rustic Acres Ltd intends to sell the aforementioned property to Shrewd. Both Crafty and Shrewd agree that they would want the transaction structured in such a manner that they pay the least amount of tax possible. With this in mind, Crafty suggests that the only way to avoid high taxation would be to create a subsidiary company, Wildflower Ltd, in which Rustic Acres Ltd would own 90% of the shares and transfer the land that is the subject of the sale to Wildflower Ltd. Once the transfer of the land to Wildflower Ltd is complete, Wildflower Ltd can then allot shares to Shrewd that would be equal to the value of the land. As such, both Rustic Acres Ltd and Shrewd would pay minimal taxes.
i. If the transaction is structured as aforementioned, both Rustic Acres Ltd and Shrewd would pay minimal taxes in comparison to a direct sale? True or False. (1 mark)
ii. Giving reasons, explain whether this would be a valid transaction. (6 marks)

a) Genga Microfinance Ltd is a microfinance institution providing accessible financial services to the rural communities participating in the blue economy sector in Western Kenya. The approach of Genga Microfinance Ltd is to obtain grants from development partners and help local communities participate effectively in the blue economy sector. The interest in the sector has grown rapidly and development partners have pumped in millions of shillings to support the sector. After operating for several years, a major audit of Genga Microfinance Ltd revealed that the Directors have been engaged in corruption. The development partners have threatened to stop their support to Genga Microfinance Ltd unless the directors are removed. The notice for the motion to remove the directors has been duly served in advance pending the convening of the special meeting to pass a special resolution to this effect. Suma is one of the directors targeted. He has approached your office to get advice on whether he has a right to protest against his removal. Advise. (6 marks)
b) Following the corruption scandal that affected Genga Microfinance Ltd. The company has lost many of its customers and also the funding secured through its partners. Its closest competitor, Bura Microfinance Ltd, has expressed interest to acquire Genga Microfinance Ltd. Genga Microfinance Ltd has conducted a thorough investigation covering all aspects of the scandal using private investigators. Some of the information unearthed is considered to be highly confidential and if revealed to the public it may be devastating to the company. However, Bura Microfinance Ltd wants a complete access to all the information the company is holding before any deal could be reached. Draft a confidentiality agreement to protect the sensitive information to be shared during negotiations between Genga Microfinance Ltd and Bura Microfinance Ltd. (4 marks)
